instruction: Walk to the living room on the far side of the counter. Stop next to the end table next to the couch with the bookshelf on your right.

text: I'm starting by examining the observe and look_around functions.

I'm getting my bearings in this space—starting from the kitchen, I need to navigate past the counter to reach the living room where there's a couch, an end table, and a bookshelf positioned on the right side. Let me take in the full layout to understand what I'm working with.

Looking at the left view at 270°, I can see the kitchen island counter with a living room beyond it—bookshelf on the right, couch on the left. That counter is the landmark I need to head toward. I should turn left 270° and navigate into that room, though I'm reconsidering my current orientation since I was initially facing the kitchen hallway.

Actually, I need to clarify the angle math here. The look_around views are at 0°, 90°, 180°, and 270°, which suggests clockwise rotation. To face the left view at 270°, I can either turn right 18 times (270÷15) or turn left 6 times (90÷15), since turning left 90° is equivalent to turning right 270°. So I'll turn left 6 times to face that room with the couch and bookshelf.

The counter is blocking my direct path forward, so I need to navigate around it. I can see the living room beyond with a couch on the left and shelving to the right, so I'll turn left slightly to find a way past the island and into that space.

Looking at the left view, I can see the dining area layout is consistent with what I observed before—chairs beyond the counter and windows visible. The ahead view confirms the living room is accessible past the counter with a couch and shelving unit. I'm thinking the best approach is to move right along the counter side where there's more open space, then turn left into the room.

I'm getting a better sense of the space now—there's a dining area with chairs and a table in the middle-left, and further right I can see a living room with a fireplace and brick wall with shelving. I want to turn left a bit more to see what's beyond the island and get a fuller picture of the room layout.
I've overshot the turn—I'm now facing the sink area after making too many left turns. I need to correct course and turn right to navigate around the island and back toward the living room.

I'm back at the dining/living room view with the island creating a tight clearance on the right at 1.0. Moving along the left side where there's more space at 1.8, I can navigate forward carefully by drifting left to avoid the island corner, even though the center is tighter at 1.4. Let me take a few steps forward to see what's ahead.

I can see the living room layout now—fireplace on the right, a white sofa in the background, and a dining table with chairs to the left. I'll head forward through the center with 3.2 meters of clearance toward the living room.

I can see the white couch in the middle-right area with a fireplace and brick wall beyond it—this is definitely the living room. I need to locate the bookshelf on the right side of the couch and an end table nearby, so I'll move closer to the couch and scan the surrounding area.
I notice the dining table blocking the way ahead, so I'm turning right toward the couch and living room area to navigate around it.

I'm looking at the living room layout now—there's a fireplace with a stone wall, a built-in shelving unit with a TV next to it, and a white couch positioned to the left. I need to locate the end table beside the couch, which should be where I'm standing when facing the couch with the bookshelf on my right.

I see the issue now—turning left just reversed the right turn, so I'm back where I started facing the dining table. That's not getting me closer to the couch. I need to try a different approach and actually move toward it rather than just rotating in place.

I can see the fireplace and built-in bookshelf now, which confirms this is the living room I'm looking for. I'll head toward the couch at the bottom left, keeping the bookshelf on my right as I move forward.

I'm visualizing the room layout: a white sectional couch on the left with an end table and lamps nearby, a built-in dark bookshelf on the right, and a fireplace in the center with a coffee table in front. The instruction is asking me to position myself next to the end table by the couch, keeping the bookshelf to my right.

Since the bookshelf is already on my right side, I'm maintaining the correct orientation as I move toward the couch's near end where those lamps are positioned. I'll turn slightly left and walk toward that end table.

The couch dominates the lower part of my view now that I'm close to it, with a small lamp-topped end table visible near the window and the stone fireplace on the right side. Since I turned left earlier, the bookshelf has rotated out of my direct line of sight and is now somewhere behind me to the right.

I need to figure out where the other end table is—the one I'm supposed to stand next to with the bookshelf on my right. The end table I see is at the far left near the window, but there might be another one at the near end of the couch where the sectional corner is. Let me look around from where I'm standing to get a better sense of the full room layout and locate both the end table and bookshelf positions.

I'm orienting myself spatially in the room—the right view at +90° shows the bookshelf prominently positioned in the center-left of that frame, which means when I face that direction, the bookshelf is directly in front of me, with a hallway opening visible beyond it. The forward view reveals a couch with an end table and lamp near a window at the far end, and the fireplace is positioned to the far right. I can see the end table with the lamp at the far end of the couch near the window, before the armchairs start. That's the target. I'm currently facing ahead, and the end table is slightly left of center in my view, so I'll move forward toward it.

I've spotted the end table with the lamp on the right side of the frame, positioned near the far end of the couch. I need to turn slightly right to face it directly and move closer, keeping an eye on the clearance—there's good space on the right at 3.2 meters, though the couch is nearby in the center at 1.4 meters.

I can see the full room layout now—the end table with the lamp is positioned at the middle-left, between the couch and armchairs, confirming the bookshelf is on the right side when facing into the room. I'm heading toward that lamp table at the near end of the couch, so I'll turn slightly left and walk forward.
I can see the end table with the lamp now—it's positioned near the center, right between the couch corner and the armchair. I need to move forward and slightly to the right to get closer to it.

The room layout is coming together nicely with the fireplace centered, bookshelf on the right, and couch on the left with the end table and lamp visible between them. I'm positioned correctly facing into the room with the bookshelf to my right. Now I'm shifting my focus to that end table near the left-center of the image, turning slightly left to approach it more closely.

I'm getting caught in a loop between the same two perspectives, so I need to commit to moving forward toward that end table with the lamp at around x=290, y=270. The center path ahead has decent clearance at 1.9 meters, so I'll move mostly straight forward with just a slight adjustment to the right.
The clearance readings haven't changed—the center distance is still 1.9m, which means I'm blocked by something ahead. I'm not moving forward as expected, likely due to an obstacle at that distance.

The image hasn't changed—I'm blocked right away, probably by furniture like a couch or coffee table in the frame. Since the right side showed 3.8 meters of open space, I'll turn that direction to navigate around the obstacle.

I've moved past that obstacle and now I'm viewing the room from a different vantage point—closer to the couch's far end with the fireplace and bookshelf coming into view ahead. I can make out an end table between the armchairs on the left side and a lamp at the far left edge, plus what looks like a white sculptural piece functioning as an end table near the bottom right corner of the couch. I'm realizing I may have actually moved past the end table's position and am now closer to the bookshelf side of the room, so I need to reorient myself relative to where the couch actually is.

The end table with the lamp was positioned near where the couch meets the sitting area with those armchairs—I saw it clearly at the far left in earlier frames. In this current view, that area is at the left edge, partially cut off, but I can still see the lamp there. I should turn left to face that lamp table and get back toward the couch.

I've gotten turned around navigating the space—I'm facing back toward the couch and hallway now, which means I've been going in circles. Let me use look_around to get my bearings straight and figure out where I actually am, keeping in mind I only have 37 tool calls left to work with.

Looking at the 180° view behind me, there's a small standalone dark bookshelf on the left side—distinct from the larger built-in one I saw earlier—which appears to be positioned near the kitchen island or in the doorway between the living room and kitchen area.

So I'm realizing there are actually two different shelving units: the big built-in shelf visible from the right (90°) near the fireplace, and this smaller standalone bookshelf in the transitional space. The task mentions "the couch with the bookshelf on your right," which matches the large sectional I've been near, with that big built-in shelf on its right side.

Now I need to locate the end table next to the couch—I recall seeing a small round table with a lamp near the armchair corner, and in the 90° view I can just make out what looks like a lamp at the far left edge of the image, likely part of that end table setup.

My plan is to turn right 90 degrees and move toward that round table with the lamp, which sits between the couch and the armchairs.

I'm noticing the round side table positioned between the armchairs near the window, though it's not directly adjacent to the couch. The couch itself appears to be at the bottom of my view with an orange pillow visible, suggesting I'm standing quite close to it. I should turn left to get a better look at the couch's end and see if there's a dedicated end table positioned right against it.
I can see the end table with the lamp now—it's at the far end of the couch near the window. Let me adjust my angle slightly to center it better and move closer to get a clearer view.

I'm locating the end table next to the couch—it's positioned at the center-left of the room, right beside the couch's end before the armchairs, with the bookshelf visible on the right side. Now I'm moving toward it to examine it more closely.

I'm getting closer to the end table by the couch, noticing the lamp is now visible in the frame near where the couch meets the armchair. The center clearance is tight at 1.6, so I'll angle right slightly to face the end table directly and move in closer.
I'm positioned very close to the end table with the lamp—it's just ahead of me at around coordinates 180, 270. I'll turn left to face it directly and step forward.

I've overturned too far left and now I'm facing the wall and couch corner. The end table with the lamp is barely visible at the far right edge. I need to pivot right a few steps to orient toward it directly and close the distance.

I'm getting a good view of the room from near the couch—the bookshelf is clearly visible on the right, and I can see the end table with the lamp positioned left-center, about 3-4 meters away. With 22 tool calls left, I need to be strategic, so I'll turn about 30° left and move toward that lamp table to examine it more closely.
